title: diplazium subnudum
intro: Diplazium subnudum, also known as the naked wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Athyriaceae. It is native to tropical regions and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Diplazium subnudum can be used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, to harness its medicinal properties.
